name: Lat Pulldown
resistance_profile: Challenges the lats through a long range, with a strong stretch at the top (arms overhead).
rom_notes: Allow a full overhead reach and slight scapular upward rotation at the top; pull to the upper chest.
cues: Start from a full overhead stretch
Drive the elbows down and in
Pull to the collarbone, not behind the neck
execution_steps: Set the thigh pad, grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, and sit with your arms fully extended overhead.
Set your chest tall with a slight lean back and brace your core.
Drive your elbows down and in, pulling the bar to your upper chest.
Squeeze the lats briefly, then return all the way up to a full overhead stretch.
good_when: You are a beginner building the vertical-pull pattern before pull-ups.
You want easily adjustable load and controllable reps for the lats.
You need a scalable substitute when bodyweight pull-ups are too hard.
bad_when: You can already do multiple strict pull-ups and want maximal loading, where weighted pull-ups progress further.
common_errors: Excessive layback / turning it into a row
